label: Slow Burn
description: Tension, longing, no physical contact beyond glances and brief accidental touches. Desire exists entirely in what is withheld.
allowedActs: intense eye contact held a beat too long
accidental touch of hands
near-kiss that doesn't land
charged conversation with layered subtext
noticing specific physical details (hands, throat, mouth) without commentary
deliberate distance maintained at effort
breath awareness in close proximity
a single held gaze that functions as a declaration
forbiddenLanguage: any explicit anatomical terms
any direct description of arousal
desire or want stated plainly — only shown
hunger used in a sexual context
any description of imagined physical acts
explicitInstruction: Build tension through restraint. The most intimate act permitted is a hand brushing a wrist — and it should feel like a threshold crossed. Every sentence that approaches physical contact should cost the character something. Leave everything to imagination. The reader's mind is the most powerful tool at this level — give it precise sensory input and let it extrapolate.
paceNote: Slow burns earn their eventual release by refusing it for as long as possible. Chapters at this level should end leaving the reader more tense than they began, not satisfied.

label: Heated
description: First kisses, heated arguments that slide toward physical awareness, sensual tension with contained expression. Contact happens; it is not pursued past a threshold.
allowedActs: kissing — from brief to drawn-out, but clothing remains
hands on face, hair, waist — purposeful, not exploratory
bodies against each other in conflict or desire without explicit intent
whispered words against skin
biting that does not draw blood in explicit context
being held against a wall or surface with clear tension
describing the feeling of a kiss in sensory detail
forbiddenLanguage: explicit anatomical names for genitalia
descriptions of sexual arousal states
explicit sexual acts
crude or clinical language for physical response
explicitInstruction: The heat exists in what is barely contained. Two characters who want each other badly but stop at the edge — or who fail to stop but fade before anything explicit — belong at level 2. The emotional content must be as present as the physical. A kiss without subtext is just choreography.
paceNote: The interruption (internal or external) is this level's primary tool. What would happen next is the question the chapter leaves open.

label: Steamy
description: Clothes stay on, or come partly off. Hands move with intent. Fade to black before full explicitness. Implied rather than shown — but the implication is specific.
allowedActs: clothing removed partially or fully suggested
hands moving over clothed or briefly described unclothed bodies
kissing down a neck, a collarbone, a shoulder
pressure and weight of bodies described without clinical detail
dialogue that makes explicit intent clear without explicit language
pre-fade descriptions of sensation and desire
the moment before the line crossed, rendered fully
forbiddenLanguage: graphic anatomical description of genitalia
explicit play-by-play of penetration or manual stimulation
crude slang
clinical pornographic language
explicitInstruction: Level 3 is the fade to black executed at its most skilled. The reader knows exactly what happens — the writing simply does not follow them through the door. The moment before the door closes should be the most charged, most specific, most sensory sentence in the scene. What is left to imagination should be so well set up that imagination has no work to do.
paceNote: The fade is a technique, not a failure of nerve. Execute it with intent.

label: Explicit
description: Detailed physical intimacy, named anatomically with deliberate language. Emotional grounding required — the physical acts must carry psychological weight.
allowedActs: explicit sexual acts described with sensory detail
anatomical terms used with intentionality (not crudely, not clinically)
prolonged intimate scenes with multiple acts
explicit description of arousal and physical response
dialogue that is explicit in both language and intent
supernatural elements (feeding, transformation, magic) woven into intimacy
forbiddenLanguage: crude slang for anatomy that reads as degrading without character-specific justification
clinical detachment — physical acts that carry no emotional charge
descriptions that objectify without interiority from at least one party
explicitInstruction: Level 4 demands that the body and the self are both present. Every physical act should carry psychological meaning. What a character does in this scene should reveal character — desire, fear, need, grief, power, love, any of these — or the explicitness serves nothing. The language should be precise, not euphemistic, not crude by default — chosen for what it reveals, not for shock.
paceNote: The scene must earn its explicitness through what preceded it. A level 4 scene arriving without established emotional stakes reads as gratuitous. Emotional context is not optional decoration — it is the load-bearing structure.

label: Unrestrained
description: Nothing withheld. Graphic, sophisticated, and psychologically complex. Complete scenes with full sensory immersion. The emotional and physical rendered without compression.
allowedActs: complete, uncompressed sexual scenes
extended intimacy with full physical and emotional detail
explicit acts of any kind between consenting adult characters
power dynamics, kink, supernatural feeding, transformation-adjacent intimacy
multi-act scenes without fade or ellipsis
explicit language at full register — crude where it serves character, precise where it serves scene
forbiddenLanguage: non-consensual acts framed positively without narrative critique
content involving minors
content designed to degrade without mutual consent and narrative framing
purely mechanical description with no psychological or emotional content
explicitInstruction: Level 5 is not the absence of craft — it is craft applied without restriction. The most common failure at this level is losing the characters inside the explicit content. The bodies matter; the people inside the bodies matter more. Every explicit scene at level 5 should reveal something about who these characters are that could not have been revealed any other way. If the scene could be removed without losing character knowledge, it has not done its job.
paceNote: Pacing at level 5 is a deliberate oscillation — intensity built, released, and rebuilt. Flat-out explicit content without rhythm exhausts the reader and numbs the scene. Use the level's freedom to go further than you could before — then pull back to interiority, to sensation, to small specific detail — before pushing forward again.
